summary: Moved Captain Falcon's Up-B implementation from assembly into `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.c` and partially decompiled the full `ftCa_SpecialHi` flow, while retaining inline assembly fallbacks for functions that still needed exact matching. The PR also refined Captain special-hi data and motion-var structs, split fighter flag byte `x2222` into direct bitfields, added missing common-data/prototype details, documented `vic` as shorthand for victim, and updated score reporting to include zero-current-score symbols.
what_changed: Replaced `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.s` with `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/ftCa_SpecialHi.c` and switched `obj_files.mk` from the asm object to the C object.

Added C implementations for many Captain Falcon Up-B callbacks and helpers, including `ftCa_SpecialHiThrow1_Coll`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_Enter`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_Anim`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_IASA`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_Phys`, `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Enter`, `ftCa_SpecialHi_Coll`, `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Anim`, `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_IASA`, `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Phys`, `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Coll`, `ftCa_SpecialHiCatch_Anim`, `ftCa_SpecialHiCatch_IASA`, `ftCa_SpecialHiCatch_Phys`, `ftCa_SpecialHiCatch_Coll`, `ftCa_SpecialHiThrow0_Anim`, `ftCa_SpecialHiThrow0_IASA`, `ftCa_SpecialHiThrow0_Coll`, and related static helpers.

Kept difficult or not-yet-cleanly-matching code paths under `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` as inline PowerPC assembly using `#pragma push`, `asm void`, and `nofralloc`, while providing readable C alternatives under the non-MUST_MATCH path.
Introduced `ftCaptainSpecialHiVars` in `union ftCaptain_MotionVars` at `fp+2340`, with `x0`, bitfields `x2_b0` through `x2_b7`, `x3`, and `Vec2 vel` for Falcon Dive state.
Changed `ftCaptain_DatAttrs.specialhi_air_var` from `f32` to `s32`, matching its use as a value copied into `fp->mv.ca.specialhi.x0`.

Updated `ftCa_SpecialHi.h` prototypes by adding `ftCa_SpecialHi_Enter` and `ftCa_SpecialAirHi_Enter` and removing unrelated `ftCa_SpecialLw_Enter` and `ftCa_SpecialAirLw_Enter` declarations from that header.
Split `Fighter` byte `x2222_flag` from `UnkFlagStruct` access into direct bitfields `x2222_b0` through `x2222_b7`, and mechanically updated uses across fighter core and several character files.
Added `p_ftCommonData->x258` to `ftCommonData`, replacing part of the opaque padding, to support decompiled aerial drift/friction calls in the Up-B physics code.
Added or refined prototypes in `ft_0877.h`, including a named `landing_lag` parameter for `ft_800D5CB0` and a declaration for `ft_8009CA0C(HSD_GObj*, HSD_GObj*)`.
Added glossary entry `vic`, short for `victim`, matching local variable naming such as `vic_fp` and `vic_gobj` in capture/throw code.
Removed the `tools/report_score.py` filter that skipped symbols with `current_score == 0`, allowing completely matched symbols to appear in score reporting.
smart_moves: Converted the object to C even though the entire file was only partially matched, using conditional inline assembly to preserve exact builds where needed instead of blocking progress on the hardest functions.

Factored repeated Up-B behavior into local helpers such as `doAirColl`, `doAirIASA`, `doCatchAnim`, and `ftCa_SpecialLw_800E550C`, making the decompiled control flow easier to compare against repeated assembly patterns.

Modeled the `fp+2340` motion-variable area as a dedicated `specialhi` struct with explicit bitfields and a `Vec2`, turning raw offset accesses like `0x2342`, `0x2344`, and `0x2348` into meaningful state fields.
Used existing engine constants and types (`M_PI_2`, `Vec2`, `HSD_GObj`, `Fighter`, `attr`) rather than preserving raw constants and offsets everywhere.

Named dat-attribute fields based on observed behavior, such as `specialhi_input_var`, `specialhi_horz_vel`, `specialhi_air_friction_mul`, `specialhi_catch_grav`, `specialhi_freefall_air_spd_mul`, and `specialhi_landing_lag`, making later move work more reusable.
Improved the global `Fighter` bitfield representation at `x2222` instead of continuing with nested `x2222_flag.bits.bN` access, which simplified many existing call sites.
Added documentation for `vic` at the same time the abbreviation appeared in code, reducing ambiguity for future reviewers and decompilers.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter action files, it is acceptable and useful to land a partial C conversion with `MUST_MATCH` inline assembly islands, as long as the build still routes through the C object and the C alternatives document the intended logic.
Repeated assembly bodies are good candidates for helper extraction; here, air collision, air IASA, catch animation transition, and victim-position sync were pulled into helpers to reduce duplication.

Motion-variable unions should be expanded when repeated raw `fp+2340` accesses reveal a move-specific layout. Even placeholder names like `x2_b1` are better than anonymous byte manipulation when bit positions are known.
Dat-attribute type corrections matter for matching. `specialhi_air_var` becoming `s32` reflects that the value is used like an integer counter/state field, not a float.
Direct bitfields for known fighter flag bytes can improve codegen and readability across many files, but require a mechanical update of all old `flag.bits.bN` references.
When a decompiled physics routine needs a previously hidden common-data field, expose just the proven offset, as with `ftCommonData.x258`, rather than over-naming surrounding padding.
Small prototype improvements, such as naming `landing_lag` in `ft_800D5CB0`, help propagate meaning from one matched function to future work.
naming_conventions: `vic` is documented as shorthand for `victim`; this PR uses names like `vic_fp` and `vic_gobj` for the grabbed/interacted fighter.
Move-specific motion variables use the pattern `fp->mv.ca.specialhi.<field>` for Captain Falcon special-hi state.
Known single-bit fighter flags at `fp+2222` are named directly as `x2222_b0`, `x2222_b1`, etc., instead of `x2222_flag.bits.b0`.
Captain Falcon special-hi dat-attribute fields use the `specialhi_` prefix and descriptive suffixes when known, for example `specialhi_landing_lag`, `specialhi_input_var`, and `specialhi_catch_grav`.
Unidentified per-move fields remain conservatively named, such as `x0`, `x2_b0`, `x2_b1`, and `specialhi_unk2`, rather than inventing semantics not proven by the diff.
Static helper names may use behavior-oriented names such as `doAirColl`, `doAirIASA`, and `doCatchAnim` for local decomp clarity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to select inline assembly for non-matching functions while keeping C versions available for understanding and WIP builds.
Wrapped inline assembly with `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` and `// clang-format off` to preserve formatting and assembler-sensitive code.
Used `nofralloc` in inline assembly functions to match the original prolog/epilog behavior.
Inserted explicit unused stack arrays under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`, such as `u8 _[8]`, `u8 _[16]`, `u8 _[24]`, or `u8 _[28]`, to reproduce stack-frame shapes in C functions.
Replaced old local labels like `.L_800E4BE4` with inline-assembly-compatible labels such as `lbl_800E4BE4`.
Promoted sdata2 constants to C constants: `float const ftCa_SpecialLw_804D9230 = 0`, `float const ftCa_SpecialLw_804D9234 = 1`, and `double const ftCa_SpecialLw_804D9238 = M_PI_2`.

Preserved exact low-level calls while adding higher-level structure, including `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftCommon_8007E2D0`, `ftCommon_8007D3A8`, `ft_80085134`, `ft_800D5CB0`, `ftCliffCommon_80081298`, and `ftCliffCommon_80081370`.
review_feedback: No review comments or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body evidence is minimal: it contains only the title, author, URL, and an embedded image, so intent beyond the diff and title is weakly evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match or clean up `ftCa_SpecialHi.c` after PR 827.
Search for future renames of `ftCa_SpecialLw_800E49FC`, `ftCa_SpecialLw_800E5128`, or `ftCa_SpecialLw_800E550C`, which appear to be SpecialHi-related despite their temporary SpecialLw-style names.
Look for later documentation or review explaining the semantics of `mv.ca.specialhi.x2_b0`, `x2_b1`, and `x2_b2`.
Search for subsequent uses or renames of `ftCommonData.x258` to determine its physics meaning.
Check later PRs for more `x2222_bN` flag naming refinements or semantic names.
